delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

summary: In this course, students will learn to write strong academic paragraphs by focusing on structure, coherence, and argumentation. Through a process-based approach, they will engage in drafting, revising, peer editing, and teacher conferencing to refine their writing. The course emphasizes different writing styles and strategies while encouraging students to develop their skills through consistent practice, both in class and independently.
aims: This course aims to equip students with the experience, skills, and strategies necessary to write clear, well-structured, and effective paragraph-level responses to common academic English test questions.
goals: Understand and apply the fundamental structure of a well-organized academic paragraph.
Develop clear topic sentences and provide logical supporting details.
Use different writing styles appropriate to academic test responses.
Improve coherence and cohesion in paragraph writing.
Engage in the writing process, including drafting, revising, and editing.
Provide constructive feedback through collaborative peer editing.
Build a portfolio demonstrating progress in academic writing.
Develop independent writing habits through regular out-of-class practice.

plan.text: Sentence Basics: Structures and Common errors. Combining sentences for clarity.

prereq: Students should be at CEFR-J A2.2 English level or higher.
prep: Students must do a significant amount of writing preparation at home. The process of writing skills development involves considerable out-of-class practice.”
teachers: ◎ デビット レスリー
